Documentation.long_help: Map an array (scalar or vector) through an adjustable piecewise-linear curve to a new scalar range. For vector arrays the magnitude is used. Select an input array, specify input and output ranges, edit the transfer curve, and the result is a new scalar point-data array.
Documentation.short_help: Map array values through an editable curve to a target scalar range.

StringVectorProperty.name: InputArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Input Array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: The point-data array to map. For vector arrays, the magnitude is used.

StringVectorProperty.name: OutputArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Output Array Name
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Name of the new scalar array that will be added to the output.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: InputRangeMin
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Input Range Min
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Minimum of the input value range.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: InputRangeMax
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Input Range Max
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Maximum of the input value range.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: OutputRangeMin
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Output Range Min
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Minimum value of the output mapped range.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: OutputRangeMax
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Output Range Max
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Maximum value of the output mapped range.

ProxyProperty.name: CurveTransferFunction
ProxyProperty.label: Transfer Curve
ProxyProperty.Documentation: Drag control points to shape the transfer curve. X = normalised input [0,1], Y = normalised output [0,1].
